ORGANIZATIONAL-LEVEL AGGREGATE ANALYSIS

======================================================================
CONCLUSION (based on synthetic demonstration)
======================================================================
The CDt index distinguishes between technologies that consolidate the status
quo (negative values) and those that destabilize it (positive values),
a dimension orthogonal to traditional citation impact (I5).
In our synthetic data, federal research funding (gov_interest) is associated
with more destabilizing patents (positive coefficient on CD5), while
commercial engagement (firm assignee) is associated with more consolidating
patents (negative coefficient on CD5). Both types of sponsorship are
positively associated with forward citation impact (I5), demonstrating that
impact measures alone cannot distinguish these fundamentally different
types of technological change.
These results mirror the paper's empirical findings with real patent data.
======================================================================
